content:
So I was going to start working on a new Platform driver which will interact with Greybus and UART. It has to run on BeaglePlay which has an ARM64 CPU. So I was just wondering what the status of AARCH64 target is. Are there any major limitations other than not being officially maintained yet?

Here is my Blog Post about more details regarding my project.

Jamie Cunliffe from Arm is taking care of upstreaming the AArch64 support. The first iteration with some more details is at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230125163739.3798252-1-Jamie.Cunliffe@arm.com/. He recently told me he intends to resume that work soon.

Does anyone know of any updates for the aarch64 patch at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230606145606.1153715-1-Jamie.Cunliffe@arm.com/T/#m9ad445621023db8730212fe1392f627a49b3b48c? I'll gently poke if not
Seems relevant after the net discussion

I have asked our contacts at Arm what is their plan/status. Given we have not heard much from the arch maintainers, it may be time to raise this at LPC too.

Of course, if someone wants to ask them in the mailing list, it may help too since others may see it that way. Though I would perhaps suggest waiting a week to see what they tell us first.
Alright, I will follow up on the list sometime next week
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20231016131523.1521965-1-Jamie.Cunliffe@arm.com/
but still using ARCH :-(
Seems work is still ongoing. Here is v4 link: https://lore.kernel.org/linux-arm-kernel/20231020155056.3495121-1-Jamie.Cunliffe@arm.com/
It's just waiting on Masahiro to ack, which is kind of unfortunate because it is stuck in limbo
Miguel suggested adding a Tested-by if you can verify it builds and the modules load
(I tried to do this but can't seem to build the kernel on my aarch64 laptop, intend to try cross compiling from x64 at some point)

Trevor Gross said:

Miguel suggested adding a Tested-by if you can verify it builds and the modules load

Hmm. Will give it a whirl if I get time. I would like to help speedup aarch64 target since that would mean I might potentially be able to write drivers in Rust.

Miguel suggested adding a Tested-by if you can verify it builds and the modules load

Just ported the patch to current tag and gave it a try on an arm64 board (just for the fun of it). Builds without warnings (cross-compiling from x64), boots with kunit doctests (all passing), sample modules load (not build in) and the out-of-tree module works as well.

Yes, using it via rust-dev since some weeks it works nicely. Sent a Tested-by.

Anything else we can do to support getting it merged?

I was wondering how this ifdef CONFIG_$ARCH approach is going to scale when there is potentially a mix of arches that use a target.json and such that do not. Barely know anything about kconfig but wouldn't that be a case for something like a ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON in the arch/$ARCH/Kconfig that is n by default?
Thanks for the TBs. I gave a gentle nudge and added the kbuild list so maybe somebody else will see it (just the maintainer was added before)

Valentin Obst said:

I was wondering how this ifdef CONFIG_$ARCH approach is going to scale when there is potentially a mix of arches that use a target.json and such that do not. Barely know anything about kconfig but wouldn't that be a case for something like a ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON in the arch/$ARCH/Kconfig that is n by default?

That's a good thought, you should mention it
Or just send a followup once it makes it upstream

That's a good thought, you should mention it

Thanks. I haven't actually tested if that would work, will do that and then send the comment.
It's gone through a few cycles so check the history in case it's mentioned

Valentin Obst said:

I was wondering how this ifdef CONFIG_$ARCH approach is going to scale when there is potentially a mix of arches that use a target.json and such that do not. Barely know anything about kconfig but wouldn't that be a case for something like a ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON in the arch/$ARCH/Kconfig that is n by default?

There are not so many architectures, and in any case they should avoid using target.json (e.g. loongarch said they will try). In any case, given the cadence of that patch series, I think it is more important to land the architecture first and then do the refactor if needed.
I'll ask Masahiro to give his ack on it.

There are not so many architectures, and in any case they should avoid using target.json (e.g. loongarch said they will try). In any case, given the cadence of that patch series, I think it is more important to land the architecture first and then do the refactor if needed.

Okay then I'll better not open a new topic for discussion. For reference, this is the patch on top of this set which changes it to use the proposed mechanism. Seems to compile.

Patch

diff --git a/arch/Kconfig b/arch/Kconfig
index c91917b50..bbb1e35df 100644
--- a/arch/Kconfig
+++ b/arch/Kconfig
@@ -369,6 +369,12 @@ config HAVE_RUST
          This symbol should be selected by an architecture if it
          supports Rust.

+config ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON
+       bool
+       help
+         This symbol should be selected by an architecture that
+         uses a 'target.json' instead of a builtin rustc target.
+
 config HAVE_FUNCTION_ARG_ACCESS_API
        bool
        help
diff --git a/arch/x86/Kconfig b/arch/x86/Kconfig
index 5edec175b..51a07b665 100644
--- a/arch/x86/Kconfig
+++ b/arch/x86/Kconfig
@@ -125,6 +125,7 @@ config X86
        select ARCH_USE_QUEUED_RWLOCKS
        select ARCH_USE_QUEUED_SPINLOCKS
        select ARCH_USE_SYM_ANNOTATIONS
+       select ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON       if X86_64
        select ARCH_WANT_BATCHED_UNMAP_TLB_FLUSH
        select ARCH_WANT_DEFAULT_BPF_JIT        if X86_64
        select ARCH_WANTS_DYNAMIC_TASK_STRUCT
diff --git a/rust/Makefile b/rust/Makefile
index cb40a1ce1..63689959d 100644
--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -437,7 +437,7 @@ $(obj)/core.o: private rustc_objcopy = $(foreach sym,$(redirect-intrinsics),--re
 $(obj)/core.o: private rustc_target_flags = $(core-cfgs)
 $(obj)/core.o: $(RUST_LIB_SRC)/core/src/lib.rs FORCE
        $(call if_changed_dep,rustc_library)
-ifdef CONFIG_X86_64
+ifdef CONFIG_ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON
 $(obj)/core.o: scripts/target.json
 endif

diff --git a/scripts/Makefile b/scripts/Makefile
index c85d130a4..9074ba52e 100644
--- a/scripts/Makefile
+++ b/scripts/Makefile
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@ hostprogs-always-$(CONFIG_SYSTEM_EXTRA_CERTIFICATE)   += insert-sys-cert
 hostprogs-always-$(CONFIG_RUST_KERNEL_DOCTESTS)                += rustdoc_test_builder
 hostprogs-always-$(CONFIG_RUST_KERNEL_DOCTESTS)                += rustdoc_test_gen

-ifdef CONFIG_X86_64
+ifdef CONFIG_ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON
 always-$(CONFIG_RUST)                                  += target.json
 filechk_rust_target = $< < include/config/auto.conf

diff --git a/scripts/generate_rust_target.rs b/scripts/generate_rust_target.rs
index 9e117957e..50dbd12d7 100644
--- a/scripts/generate_rust_target.rs
+++ b/scripts/generate_rust_target.rs
@@ -148,8 +148,8 @@ fn main() {
     let mut ts = TargetSpec::new();

     // `llvm-target`s are taken from `scripts/Makefile.clang`.
-    if cfg.has("ARM64") {
-        panic!("arm64 uses the builtin rustc aarch64-unknown-none target");
+    if !cfg.has("ARCH_WANT_RUST_TARGET_JSON") {
+        panic!("Target architecture uses the builtin rustc target.");
     } else if cfg.has("X86_64") {
         ts.push("arch", "x86_64");
         ts.push(
Yes, please wait for this patch to land before sending any modifications to it.
I think the approach proposed by Masahiro is a bit cleaner than what I proposed above as it avoids setting KBUILD_RUSTFLAGS in the arch Makefiles. Would send a cleanup patch when this one is merged.
Hm, actually, since loongarch will be switching to builtin it would be for x86 only. Maybe not worth it.
Are there any news on getting aarch64 merged? Or anything we can help with? Several people sent their Tested-by some weeks ago but since then I don't see any update.
It has an ack from everybody needed, but now it needs a change

Jamie Cunliffe hasn't sent anything to any lists since October, and I don't see that name on Zulip. Maybe if you're interested, send them an email and ask if you (or someone) could pick it up? Or maybe Jamie would do a new version but doesn't know it's needed
It used to be blocked by Masahiro, but now Masahiro has responded.
We are talking about https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/CAK7LNATknuOCweRaQcsvw6k7vVQVVtQnbc=Q-NX-EA3J5yzZAA@mail.gmail.com/ correct?
Yeah.
Thanks for reaching out Dirk
Applied, woohoo!
Yeah! Thanks! :grinning:
